module_map.responsibility: Orchestrates lsd workflow: initializes theme/colors/icons, fetches file metadata, applies sorting, delegates to display
Renders file listings: grid/tree layout, column alignment, padding, header rendering, hyperlink support. Central formatting hub.
Metadata collection and storage. Central data structure carrying all file information through pipeline.
CLI argument parsing and validation. Defines user-configurable display options.
Color resolution: maps file attributes to ANSI colors using lscolors, theme configuration, git status.
Icon resolution: selects icons based on file type, extension, or name. TTY and encoding aware.
Theme configuration loading and caching. Supports default, lscolors, custom JSON themes.
Sorting implementation: comparators for size, date, name, extension, version, inode, links, accessed time.

data_flow.description: Parse CLI args into Flags. Initialize Colors (theme-aware), Icons (TTY/encoding-aware), GitTheme. Set up sort functions.
For each path: read symlink_metadata(), resolve symlinks, extract Name (with hyperlinks), Date, Owner, Permissions, FileType, Size, INode, Links. Recursive via recurse_into().
Apply sort functions (Name, Size, Date, Inode, Links, Version, Extension, AccessTime) as configured in Flags.sorting. Returns sorted Meta vector.
For each Meta.name/size/date/owner: map file attributes (type, permissions, git_status, extension) to ANSI style via lscolors/theme.
Select icon for each file: by extension, by name, or default. Return Unicode or nerd-font icon.
For each Meta and each Block (Name, Size, Date, Owner, Permissions, INode, Links, GitStatus): call Meta.render(colors, icons) → ColoredString. Collect into Cell.
Calculate padding per column (detect_size_lengths), add header, create term_grid Grid, add cells with padding rules, render to string. Respect terminal width.

cross_module_hubs.reason: Central formatting hub. Imports: Colors, Icons, Meta, Flags, GitTheme, Block, FileType, DisplayOption, term_grid. Exports: grid(), tree() public API. Called by core.rs::display(). Uses all other modules for rendering.

Central data structure. Meta struct passed through entire pipeline. Depends on 10+ submodules (name, date, size, owner, permissions, filetype, symlink, indicator, inode, links, access_control, git_file_status). Imported by core, display, sort, color.
Configuration source. Flags struct read by core, display, sort, meta, color, icon. Defines all user-configurable behavior. No outbound deps (leaf module in dependency tree).

change_proposal.title: Add SHA-256 file checksum display column
change_proposal.description: Add SHA-256 hash computation and display as optional Block alongside Name, Size, Date, etc.
files_to_modify: src/meta/mod.rs - Add hash field to Meta struct
src/meta/hash.rs - NEW FILE - Hash type with from_path() and render()
src/display.rs - Handle Block::Hash in inner_display_grid/tree cell rendering
src/color.rs - Add Elem::Hash for color styling (optional)
new_types: meta/hash.rs: pub struct Hash { hash: String } - wraps hex SHA-256 digest
meta/hash.rs: impl Hash { pub fn from_path(path: &Path) -> io::Result<Self> } - computes hash via sha2 crate
flags/blocks.rs: Block::Hash - new enum variant with display string 'hash' or 'checksum'
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow meta/size.rs pattern: struct wraps computed value, impl has from_path() constructor, render() method returns ColoredString. Use lazy_static or OnceCell to cache hash computation for deduplicated files. Integrate into Block enum dispatch in display.rs.
change_proposal.integration_point: In display.rs::inner_display_grid/tree, add match arm for Block::Hash. Call meta.hash.render(colors, icons) → ColoredString. Treat as fixed-width column (64 chars for SHA-256 hex). Add optional --blocks hash flag parsing in flags/blocks.rs.
change_proposal.architectural_fit: Follows existing pattern: Meta struct holds computed value, render() called during display. No changes to core data flow (fetch, sort). Hash computation is I/O-bound; consider caching or async for large recursive listings.
risks: PERFORMANCE: SHA-256 hashing all files adds I/O overhead. Recursive (-R) on large trees will be slow. Mitigate: make --blocks hash optional, cache hashes, offer async variant.
COLOR STYLING: Need to define Elem::Hash if coloring hashes by type/status. May require theme/color.rs updates.
COLUMN WIDTH: Fixed 64-char width may not fit narrow terminals. Consider truncation or wrapping logic (see detect_size_lengths pattern).
SYMLINKS: Should --dereference flag affect hash computation (hash link target vs. link itself)? Document behavior.
CACHING: If same file appears in multiple lists or recursive walks, hash computed multiple times. Consider Arc<Mutex<HashMap>> in Core.
ERROR HANDLING: File permission errors during hash (unreadable files) must be handled gracefully (similar to permission display errors).

data_flow_diagram.description: File entry journey through lsd pipeline
steps: User invokes lsd with Flags (--long, --blocks size,date,name, --sort name, -R)
Core::new() → Colors, Icons, GitTheme, sort functions
Core::fetch() → Vec<Meta> via Meta::from_path() for each path
Core::sort() → apply SortFn comparators to Meta.size, Meta.date, Meta.name
inner_display_grid() loops Meta → for each Block in Flags.blocks

tool_usage.purpose: Structural overview: file tree, function counts; detailed inspection of key modules and their exports/imports
Read source to understand function signatures, type declarations, module dependencies
Pattern search for key function calls and type usage
